  • Contact

    • The Monitoring Blind Spot

    30–40% of critical rotating equipment has no condition monitoring. Discover what you can't see — and what it costs.

    What's Being Missed?

    Equipment hidden from view is hidden from insight. Until it's too late.

    🔴 Submerged Pumps

    Cannot be monitored visually or with standard accelerometers. Bearing wear and cavitation go undetected until failure.

    🔴 Enclosed Motors

    Sealed enclosures and ATEX certification prevent access. Equipment runs blind, with no early warning of degradation.

    🔴 Subsea Equipment

    Deepwater installations have zero visibility into bearing health and structural integrity. Failure means emergency response and mission loss.

    🔴 Gearboxes in Tight Spaces

    Integrated into systems where sensors cannot be mounted. Gear tooth damage, pitting, and lubrication issues stay hidden.

    Why This Matters

    Every hidden failure carries costs you can see — and costs you cannot.

    When equipment fails without warning, production stops. For manufacturers, refineries, and utilities, every hour of unplanned downtime translates to lost revenue, missed delivery windows, and customer penalties. A single bearing failure can idle an entire production line — and the repair lead time for specialized equipment can stretch weeks. This is the cost everyone sees.

    Unexpected equipment failure doesn't just stop production. It creates hazardous conditions. A catastrophic pump failure can cause flooding, chemical release, or personnel injury. In offshore and subsea operations, failure means emergency response and potential loss of life. Unmonitored equipment is uncontrolled risk — and regulators are increasingly demanding visibility.

    Equipment doesn't always fail gently. When a bearing fails, it can damage the shaft, housing, or coupled equipment. When seals break, contamination spreads to the entire fluid system. What starts as a localized bearing issue becomes a repair requiring full system overhaul — turning a 5,000 EUR replacement into a 50,000 EUR project. Without early warning, you pay for cascading failure, not prevention.

    Because failure is unpredictable, operations keep large inventories of expensive spare parts on hand — just in case. For critical equipment with long lead times, this means millions in idle capital. Predictive maintenance cuts spare parts inventory by 25–40%, freeing capital and reducing storage costs. Without visibility, you're paying to be reactive instead of proactive.

    Offshore, marine, and hazardous-area regulations increasingly require documented condition monitoring. Failure to monitor — or inability to prove you're monitoring — creates compliance gaps, audit findings, and potential operation restrictions. Regulators don't accept "we didn't see it coming" as an excuse.

    The Numbers

    30–40%
    of critical rotating equipment globally has no condition monitoring
    $50M+
    average annual loss per large operation from unplanned equipment failure
    3–5x
    cost multiplier when cascading damage occurs
    25–40%
    reduction in spare parts inventory with predictive maintenance

    The Solution: Monitor What Was Invisible

    Samotics' Electrical Signature Analysis (ESA) provides continuous condition monitoring for equipment that conventional sensors cannot reach.

    Non-Invasive

    No sensors to mount, no equipment modification. Monitor submerged, enclosed, and sealed equipment without opening systems or risking contamination.

    Early Detection

    Detect bearing degradation, cavitation, and mechanical issues weeks before failure. Act before risk becomes crisis.

    Proven Across Industries

    Deployed in offshore, marine, power generation, refining, and manufacturing. Works where traditional monitoring can't.

    Integrated with Your Systems

    Feeds directly into your CMMS, control systems, and dashboards. Actionable insights, not raw data.

    Ready to See What You've Been Missing?

    Talk to our team about how ESA can eliminate your monitoring blind spots and reduce unplanned downtime.
